title: The adapter encrypts at the app layer with AES-256-GCM derived from the user's wallet signature
content:
## Claim

The Bee Reactor Adapter performs **app-layer AES-256-GCM encryption before upload** rather than relying on Swarm-native encryption, to control keys and keep references 32 bytes.

## Evidence

Key derivation chain:
```
wallet.personal_sign(msg) -> keccak256 -> secp256k1 private key
  -> SHA-256 -> AES-256 symmetric key
```

Ciphertext format:
```
[SWE prefix (3 bytes)] [IV (12 bytes)] [ciphertext + GCM auth tag]
```

Advantages over Swarm-native (`Swarm-Encrypt: true`):
- Full control over key management
- Reference stays 32 bytes (no embedded 32-byte decryption key)
- Compatible with any storage backend, not just Swarm
- Deterministic: same wallet = same key on any device

Sharing today uses `SHA-256(sender_address:recipient_address)` — both parties derive the same key independently (but this is non-revocable; ACT is the planned replacement).

## Why it matters

App-layer encryption decouples the document-level security story from the Swarm transport. The deterministic wallet-derived key enables cross-device recovery without any server-side key escrow.
description: Wallet personal_sign -> keccak256 -> secp256k1 private key -> SHA-256 -> AES-256 key. Format: [SWE prefix][IV][ciphertext+GCM tag]. Keeps references 32 bytes.
